mission.title: Install and Configure Knative on Kubernetes
mission.description: Knative Serving is a Kubernetes-based framework that enables the deployment and serving of applications and functions as serverless containers. It allows for rapid deployment, automatic scaling, and advanced routing capabilities, making it a powerful tool for modern cloud-native applications.

steps.title: Add the Knative Serving Helm repository
steps.description:
Add the official Knative Serving Helm chart repository to your Helm configuration:
```bash
helm repo add knative https://charts.knative.dev
helm repo update
```

steps.title: Install Knative Serving
steps.description:
Install Knative Serving using Helm, creating a namespace called 'knative-serving':
```bash
helm install knative-serving knative/serving --namespace knative-serving --create-namespace --version knative-v1.21.1
```

steps.title: Verify the installation
steps.description:
Check that the Knative Serving components are running:
```bash
kubectl get pods --namespace knative-serving
```

steps.title: Configure resource limits
steps.description:
Set resource limits for the Knative Serving deployment by editing the configuration:
```bash
kubectl edit configmap config-autoscaler --namespace knative-serving
```

steps.title: Set up monitoring (optional)
steps.description:
Install Prometheus for monitoring Knative Serving:
```bash
kubectl apply -f https://github.com/knative/serving/releases/download/knative-v1.21.1/monitoring.yaml
```

uninstall.title: Remove the Helm release
uninstall.description:
Uninstall the Knative Serving release:
```bash
helm uninstall knative-serving --namespace knative-serving
```

uninstall.title: Delete the Knative Serving namespace
uninstall.description:
Remove the namespace created for Knative Serving:
```bash
kubectl delete namespace knative-serving
```

uninstall.title: Clean up CRDs
uninstall.description:
Delete the Custom Resource Definitions associated with Knative Serving:
```bash
kubectl delete crd $(kubectl get crd | grep 'serving.knative.dev' | awk '{print $1}')
```

upgrade.title: Backup current state
upgrade.description:
Export the current configuration and resources:
```bash
kubectl get all --namespace knative-serving -o yaml > knative-backup.yaml
```

upgrade.title: Update the Helm repository
upgrade.description:
Make sure your Helm repository is up to date:
```bash
helm repo update
```

upgrade.title: Upgrade Knative Serving
upgrade.description:
Upgrade Knative Serving to the latest version:
```bash
helm upgrade knative-serving knative/serving --namespace knative-serving --version knative-v1.21.1
```

upgrade.title: Verify the upgrade
upgrade.description:
Check that the upgraded components are running:
```bash
kubectl get pods --namespace knative-serving
```

troubleshooting.title: Pods stuck in Pending state
troubleshooting.description:
This may indicate insufficient resources in your cluster. Diagnose with:
```bash
kubectl describe pod <pod-name> --namespace knative-serving
```
Ensure your cluster has enough CPU and memory resources available.

troubleshooting.title: Knative services not routing traffic
troubleshooting.description:
Check the status of the Knative service:
```bash
kubectl get ksvc <service-name> --namespace knative-serving
```
Ensure the service is in 'Ready' state and check for any configuration errors.

troubleshooting.title: Autoscaler not scaling down
troubleshooting.description:
Verify the autoscaler configuration:
```bash
kubectl get configmap config-autoscaler --namespace knative-serving -o yaml
```
Check the 'minScale' setting and adjust it if necessary.

troubleshooting.title: Error in Knative Serving logs
troubleshooting.description:
Check the logs of the Knative Serving components:
```bash
kubectl logs <pod-name> --namespace knative-serving
```
Look for specific error messages to diagnose the issue.

resolution.summary: A successful installation of Knative Serving will have all components running in the 'knative-serving' namespace, and you will be able to deploy and serve applications seamlessly. You can verify the installation by checking the status of the pods and accessing the services you've deployed.
codeSnippets:
helm repo add knative https://charts.knative.dev
helm repo update
helm install knative-serving knative/serving --namespace knative-serving --create-namespace --version knative-v1.21.1

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running version 1.24 or higher, and have Helm and kubectl installed and configured.
